text.title: ArgoCD GitOps: Core Rules
text.summary: Separate repos rule, folder-over-branches rule, sync wave ordering, sync windows for production.

rule.statement: Keep application source code and Kubernetes manifests in separate Git repositories; CI updates the manifests repo after building and pushing the image.
rule.rationale: Separate repos break the CI/ArgoCD trigger loop and enforce a clean separation of concerns: the app repo owns code and tests, the GitOps repo owns deployment state. This also allows different access controls: developers push to the app repo; only CI and platform team push to the GitOps repo.

rule.statement: Use Kustomize overlays or Helm value files with separate directories for each environment (e.g., `apps/myapp/overlays/staging`, `apps/myapp/overlays/production`) — never use Git branches for environment separation.
rule.rationale: Folders in the same branch allow side-by-side diff of staging vs production configuration. Branch-based environments require cross-branch comparisons that are difficult in Git and error-prone during promotion.

rule.statement: Enable `automated.selfHeal: true` for staging environments to auto-revert manual cluster changes; evaluate carefully for production where some manual interventions (emergency patches) must not be reverted.
rule.rationale: Self-healing enforces Git as source of truth by reverting any drift. In production, self-heal should be enabled but teams must have a documented emergency procedure for temporarily pausing ArgoCD sync during incident response (use sync windows or application suspend).

rule.statement: Configure sync windows to block automated syncs to production during weekends and high-traffic periods; use deny windows rather than allow windows for flexibility.
rule.rationale: Deny windows block syncs during defined periods while allowing syncs at all other times. Allow windows require defining every valid sync slot, which is overly restrictive and often leads to teams bypassing ArgoCD for urgent deployments.

rule.statement: Set `syncOptions: [ServerSideApply=true]` for all applications to use Kubernetes server-side apply instead of client-side apply, avoiding field manager conflicts on shared resources.
rule.rationale: Client-side apply writes the full object into the `kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration` annotation. Multiple tools managing the same resource (ArgoCD + Helm operator) cause apply conflicts. Server-side apply uses field managers to resolve ownership, eliminating these conflicts.

rule.statement: Use sync wave annotations (`argocd.argoproj.io/sync-wave`) to order resource deployment within an application: CRDs (-2), Namespaces (-1), ConfigMaps/Secrets/ServiceAccounts (0), StatefulSets/Databases (1), Deployments (2), Services/Ingress (3).
rule.rationale: Without sync waves, ArgoCD applies all resources simultaneously. If a Deployment is applied before its ConfigMap exists, it starts with missing configuration. Sync waves ensure prerequisites are healthy before dependents are created.
